content: {"perspective": "Industry", "answer": "The search results recover one large-scale published real-world study on IF app retention and outcomes, plus numerous marketing and company-facing pages. Source 1 and Source 4 (JMIR / ResearchGate preprint) report a 52-week observational study of 2 IF mobile apps quantifying user retention, fasting patterns, and weight loss — this is the direct academic evidence base. Source 4 specifically notes 'By week 26, users lost over 1% of their starting weight per EFH per day on average' (EFH = eligible fasting hours), providing a concrete real-world weight-loss signal in an unsupervised consumer cohort. Source 6 (PMC11091450) is a separate real-world observational study of the SIMPLE mobile application, demonstrating weight-loss effectiveness in adults with overweight and obesity in a real-world setting — direct validation that the RCT effect sizes do translate at least partially to self-paying consumers. Source 8 (Alibaba product insights commentary) offers the single numerical retention benchmark in the snippet set: 'Zero's 30-day retention is slightly lower at 57%, yet holds at 39% at 180 days' — giving concrete day-30 (57%) and day-180 (39%) retention figures, though the source is a competitive-intelligence vendor page, not a peer-reviewed or company-disclosed data point. Sources 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, and the Fastic/Simple pages (Sources 11-15) are marketing and media coverage — they confirm Zero markets itself as the 'world's most popular intermittent fasting app' (Source 3), confirm Fastic targets weight-loss outcomes, and provide the Everyday Health review of Simple (Source 15). Information gap: the snippets do NOT recover subscription churn signals tied to the AHA 2024 CV-mortality news cycle specifically, do NOT recover day-90 retention by protocol (16:8 TRE vs 5:2 vs ADF), and do NOT recover investor-deck or company-disclosed MAU figures for any IF app. The real-world evidence that IS present is directionally consistent with the RCT literature — modest but real weight loss at 6 months in self-paying consumers.", "key_findings": ["JMIR / PMC9579929 52-week observational study of 2 IF apps (Sources 1, 4): users lost over 1% of starting weight per eligible fasting hour per day by week 26 — direct real-world weight-loss evidence from an unsupervised consumer cohort.", "SIMPLE app real-world study (Source 6, PMC11091450): demonstrates weight-loss effectiveness in adults with overweight and obesity in a real-world setting — independent real-world validation of a second major IF app.", "Alibaba product-insights commentary (Source 8) quotes concrete Zero retention: 57% at day 30 and 39% at day 180 — the only specific retention numbers recovered, though the source is a competitive vendor, not peer-reviewed or company-disclosed.", "Zero markets itself as the 'world's most popular intermittent fasting app' (Source 3, Google Play) and appears in Women's Health, Fortune, Men's Health, and The Joe Rogan Experience.", "Information gap: no source recovers (a) subscription churn tied to the AHA 2024 CV-mortality news cycle, (b) day-90 retention broken down by protocol type, or (c) investor-deck or company-disclosed MAU figures for any IF app."], "sources_used": ["https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9579929/", "https://apps.apple.com/sk/app/zero-intermittent-fasting/id1168348542", "https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.zerofasting.zero&hl=en_US", "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/362290711_Retention_Fasting_Patterns_and_Weight_Loss_With_an_Intermittent_Fasting_App_A_Large-scale_52-week_Observational_Study_Preprint", "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11091450/", "https://www.alibaba.com/product-insights/is-intermittent-fasting-app-fatburner-really-better-than-zero-for-long-term-adherence-and-results.html", "https://zerolongevity.com/blog/your-first-3-months-with-zero-what-to-expect/", "https://fastic.com/en", "https://www.everydayhealth.com/diet-nutrition/simple-life-app-review/"]}
